message:
feat(core): add adaptive frame pacing governor to resize scheduler

Extend ResizeSchedulerConfig with a vsync-aware pacing governor that
dynamically adjusts the per-frame resize budget based on hitch detection
and recovery, preventing frame time spikes during rapid resize storms.

New config fields (9 parameters):
- pacing_governor_enabled, pacing_min/max_budget_units for budget range
- pacing_hitch_penalty_units, pacing_recovery_step_units for AIMD-style
  budget adjustment (additive increase, multiplicative decrease)
- pacing_recovery_frames: hitch-free frames before budget increases
- pacing_nominal/min/max_refresh_hz: vsync scaling baseline and bounds

ScheduleFrameResult enrichment:
- requested_frame_budget_units, pacing_budget_units,
  vsync_adjusted_budget_units for full budget pipeline visibility
- vsync_refresh_hz, vsync_interval_us for display timing context
- hitch_detected, hitch_overrun_units for frame timing diagnostics

ResizeSchedulerMetrics additions:
- hitch_frames, current/max_hitch_streak for hitch tracking
- pacing_adjusted_frames, budget_increase/decrease_events for
  governor activity monitoring
- last_requested/pacing/vsync budget snapshots for debugging

Also includes:
- screen.rs: decompose cursor equality assertion into per-field checks
  (x, y, shape, visibility) for clearer test failure diagnostics
- .beads/issues.jsonl: status updates for tracked issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
